description: The Changling Tomb, the largest tomb complex among the Ming Dynasty imperial mausoleums, is the burial site of Emperor Yongle Zhu Di.
The central square of Beijing, the capital of the People's Republic of China, is the largest city square in the world.
The Olympic swimming competition venue, featuring a unique membrane structure, is now the Ice Cube Ice Sports Center.
The main stadium of the 2008 Olympic Games, a masterpiece of modern architectural art and a symbol of the Olympic spirit.
A fashion landmark in Beijing, a gathering place for international brands and a hub of modern urban nightlife.
One of Beijing's most famous shopping streets, a century-old street blending traditional and modern commercial culture.
A top-tier science and engineering university in China, known for its beautiful campus and strong academic atmosphere.
Beijing's arts and leisure district, featuring creative boutiques, cafes, and specialty restaurants.
A comprehensive showcase of Beijing's history and culture, a modern museum rich in cultural relics.
An internationally renowned contemporary art district, a creative cultural park transformed from former industrial factories.
The highest point along Beijing's central axis, offering the best panoramic view of the Forbidden City, and a Ming Dynasty imperial garden.
A commercial district with over 500 years of history, preserving the traditional hutong style and old Beijing commercial culture.
